title: Effective Federal Funds Rate (DFF) — Daily Observation
summary: The Effective Federal Funds Rate (EFFR) stood at 3.64% as of 2026-04-09, unchanged from the prior observation on 2026-04-08. The rate has remained flat at 3.64% for the five consecutive trading days from April 5 through April 9, 2026.
key_points: Rate was constant at 3.64% for all five observations from 2026-04-05 through 2026-04-09
No directional change or policy shift indicated in this data snapshot
why_it_matters: The EFFR is the primary policy rate the Federal Reserve uses to influence short-term interest rates and overall monetary conditions. This flat reading indicates the Fed held rates steady during this period, consistent with the current rate range of 3.25%–3.50% target range established in late 2024.
macro_context: The flat EFFR at 3.64% suggests no acute funding market stress and no change to the Fed's stance. This is consistent with the post-2024 rate cycle where the Fed transitioned from active hiking to a holding pattern.

While the EFFR is relevant to macro catalysts, a single rate snapshot without analysis does not constitute a meaningful research record.

notes: This is a raw quantitative data snapshot from FRED, not a policy document. The content is minimal — a single rate observation with no narrative, commentary, or contextual framing.
